journal_entry.description: Acquisition of land with building for factory construction, financed by instalment note payable
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS, the land is recorded at the present value of the instalment payments using the market interest rate of 6%. The purchase price of $60,000,000 paid in 6 equal annual instalments of $10,000,000 has a present value of $49,173,240 (calculated as $10,000,000 × 4.91732, the present value of an ordinary annuity factor for 6 periods at 6%). The existing building to be demolished is not separately recognized as its value is subsumed in the land cost—demolition costs of buildings acquired with land for redevelopment are capitalized to land under IAS 16. The liability is recorded at present value with the discount of $10,826,760 representing future interest expense to be recognized over the payment period. Land is a non-depreciable asset. No tax applies to real property acquisitions in Canada (land transfers are typically exempt from HST/GST).

output.reason:
Under IFRS, the land acquisition is recorded at present value. The debit side records:
1. Land (asset increase) - The land is acquired for the purpose of constructing a new factory. The existing building to be demolished is part of the land cost, not a separate asset, since it has no future economic benefit and will be removed immediately. This is recorded at the present value of the payment obligation ($10M × 6 years at 6%).
The difference between face value ($60M) and present value creates a discount that will be unwound over time through interest expense, but that discount is a contra-liability account on the CREDIT side of this initial acquisition entry, not the debit side.
Therefore, only ONE debit line: Land = asset increase.

debit_classifier.reason:
Under IFRS, the land acquisition is recorded at present value. The debit side records:
1. Land (asset increase) - The land is acquired for the purpose of constructing a new factory. The existing building to be demolished is part of the land cost, not a separate asset, since it has no future economic benefit and will be removed immediately. This is recorded at the present value of the payment obligation ($10M × 6 years at 6%).
The difference between face value ($60M) and present value creates a discount that will be unwound over time through interest expense, but that discount is a contra-liability account on the CREDIT side of this initial acquisition entry, not the debit side.
Therefore, only ONE debit line: Land = asset increase.

output.reason:
This transaction involves acquiring land for $60,000,000 paid via instalments. On the credit side:
1. Cash payment is NOT made immediately - instead, a liability is created for the deferred payment obligation. The present value of 6 annual instalments of $10,000,000 at 6% interest represents a liability increase (financing liability/notes payable).
2. Under IFRS, when payment terms differ from immediate cash payment, the asset is recorded at present value and a financial liability is recognized. The liability increase is the credit entry.

Since the payment is deferred with instalments, this creates one credit line: liability increase for the financing obligation (present value of the instalment payments).

Credit tuple: (1,0,0,0,0,0) - one liability increase line.

output.reason: Entry correctly applies IFRS 9 and IAS 16 for asset acquisition with deferred payment terms. Land recorded at present value of $49,173,240 (PV of 6 payments of $10M at 6% = $10M × 4.91732 annuity factor). Note Payable correctly recorded at same present value. Balance verified: debits ($49,173,240) = credits ($49,173,240). Building to be demolished appropriately subsumed in land cost per IAS 16. No tax on land transfers in Canada. Directionality correct: Land (asset) debited, Note Payable (liability) credited. The $10,826,760 discount will be unwound as interest expense over the payment period through subsequent entries.
